message.content:
{
  "document_metadata": {
    "board": "CBSE",
    "subject": "Mathematics",
    "class": "IX",
    "grade": "9",
    "stream": "",
    "academic_year": "2026-27",

  },
  "classes": [
    {
      "class_name": "Class IX",
      "subject": "Mathematics",
      "part_or_section": "Curriculum Structure and Question Paper Design",
      "units": [
        {
          "unit_id": "U1",
          "unit_name": "Number System",
          "marks": 7,
          "headings": [
            "Introduction to rational numbers",
            "Representation of rational numbers on the number line",
            "Density of rational numbers and its proof",
            "Finding rational numbers between any two rational numbers",
            "Decimal representation of rational numbers",
            "Introduction to irrational numbers",
            "Proof of irrationality of √2 and √3",
            "The square root spiral"
          ],
          "possible_chapters": [
            "Number System"
          ],
          "topics": [
            "Rational Numbers",
            "Irrational Numbers",
            "Density of Rational Numbers",
            "Decimal Representation",
            "Square Root Spiral"
          ],
          "subtopics": [
            "Introduction to rational numbers",
            "Representation on number line",
            "Proof of density",
            "Finding numbers between two rationals",
            "Decimal representation computation",
            "Concept of irrationality",
            "Proofs for √2 and √3",
            "Construction of square root spiral"
          ]
        },
        {
          "unit_id": "U2",
          "unit_name": "Algebra",
          "marks": 20,
          "headings": [
            "Introduction to Polynomials",
            "Sequences and Progressions",
            "Exploring Algebraic Identities",
            "Linear Equations in Two Variables"
          ],
          "possible_chapters": [
            "Introduction to Polynomials",
            "Sequences and Progressions",
            "Exploring Algebraic Identities",
            "Linear Equations in Two Variables"
          ],
          "topics": [
            "Polynomials",
            "Algebraic Expressions",
            "Sequences and Patterns",
            "Arithmetic and Geometric Progressions",
            "Fractals and Tower of Hanoi",
            "Algebraic Identities",
            "Factorisation",
            "Linear Equations"
          ],
          "subtopics": [
            "Definition, degree, terms, coefficients",
            "Linear growth/decay modelling",
            "Slope and y-intercept (y = ax + b)",
            "Graphing linear equations",
            "Explicit vs Recursive rules",
            "nth term of AP and GP",
            "Applications in fractals",
            "Tower of Hanoi puzzle",
            "Visualising identities with geometric models",
            "Factorisation using tiles",
            "Simplifying rational expressions",
            "Graphical method for pairs of equations",
            "Consistency and inconsistency",
            "Substitution and elimination methods"
          ]
        },
        {
          "unit_id": "U3",
          "unit_name": "Coordinate Geometry",
          "marks": 4,
          "headings": [
            "Brief history of coordinate geometry",
            "The 2-D Cartesian coordinate system",
            "Distance between two points in the 2-D plane",
            "Midpoint of the line-segment"
          ],
          "possible_chapters": [
            "Coordinate Geometry"
          ],
          "topics": [
            "Cartesian System",
            "Distance Formula",
            "Midpoint Formula"
          ],
          "subtopics": [
            "History of coordinate geometry",
            "2-D Cartesian system basics",
            "Computing distance between points",
            "Determining collinearity using coordinates",
            "Finding midpoint position",
            "Checking for right-angled triangles via coordinates"
          ]
        },
        {
          "unit_id": "U4",
          "unit_name": "Geometry",
          "marks": 25,
          "headings": [
            "Introduction to Euclid's Geometry: Axioms and Postulates",
            "Lines and Angles",
            "Triangles – Congruence Theorems",
            "4-gons (Quadrilaterals)",
            "Circles"
          ],
          "possible_chapters": [
            "Introduction to Euclid's Geometry",
            "Lines and Angles",
            "Congruence of Triangles",
            "Properties of Quadrilaterals",
            "Geometry of Circles"
          ],
          "topics": [
            "Euclidean Axioms",
            "Angles and Lines",
            "Triangle Congruence",
            "Quadrilateral Properties",
            "Circle Theorems"
          ],
          "subtopics": [
            "History of geometry (India, Egypt, Greece)",
            "Baudhayana's Sulbasutras constructions",
            "Euclid's 5 postulates and definitions",
            "Rays, measures, intersecting lines",
            "Linear pair theorem, vertically opposite angles",
            "Parallelism transitivity",
            "SAS, SSS, ASA, RHS, AAS congruence conditions",
            "Isosceles triangle properties",
            "Propositions and converses (counter-examples)",
            "Parallelogram theorems and proofs",
            "Midpoint theorem for triangles and quadrilaterals",
            "Symmetry in 4-gons",
            "Circle definitions (chord, diameter, radius, arc)",
            "Angles subtended by chords/arcs",
            "Cyclic points and quadrilaterals",
            "Concentricity of circumcircles"
          ]
        },
        {
          "unit_id": "U5",
          "unit_name": "Mensuration",
          "marks": 14,
          "headings": [
            "Area and Perimeter",
            "Surface Area and Volume"
          ],
          "possible_chapters": [
            "Perimeter of Shapes",
            "Heron's Formula",
            "Brahmagupta's Formula",
            "Circumference and Arc Length",
            "Area of Sectors and Segments",
            "Surface Area and Volume of Solids"
          ],
          "topics": [
            "Perimeter and Circumference",
            "Areas (Triangles, Rectangles, Parallelograms)",
            "Heron's Formula",
            "Brahmagupta's Formula",
            "Area of Circle Sectors",
            "Surface Area and Volume"
          ],
          "subtopics": [
            "Perimeter of shapes and circles",
            "Introduction to Pi (Archimedes, Aryabhata)",
            "Length of an arc",
            "Heron's formula derivation and application",
            "Squaring a rectangle (Baudhayana)",
            "Area of circle sector/segment",
            "Generalisation vs Special case in formulas",
            "Surface area/volume of Cuboids, Cubes, Cylinders, Cones, Pyramids, Spheres"
          ]
        },
        {
          "unit_id": "U6",
          "unit_name": "Statistics and Probability",
          "marks": 10,
          "headings": [
            "Graphical representation of data",
            "Measures of central tendency",
            "Concept of probability and randomness"
          ],
          "possible_chapters": [
            "Statistics",
            "Probability"
          ],
          "topics": [
            "Data Representation",
            "Central Tendency (Mean, Median, Mode)",
            "Empirical Probability",
            "Theoretical Probability"
          ],
          "subtopics": [
            "Collecting and organising data",
            "Weighted average computation",
            "Stacked bar graphs interpretation",
            "Probability scale usage",
            "Randomness concept",
            "Tree diagrams and tables for probability",
            "Sample space and events"
          ]
        }
      ]
    }
  ],
  "formative_only_content": [
    {
      "title": "Continuous and Holistic Assessment",
      "source_text": "Continuous and holistic assessment through projects, activities, and investigations forms an integral part of the learning process, moving beyond summative examinations.",
      "related_unit": "",
      "related_chapter": "",
      "assessment_status": "formative"
    },
    {
      "title": "Portfolio Assessment",
      "source_text": "Portfolio 05 Marks (Internal Assessment)",
      "related_unit": "",
      "related_chapter": "",
      "assessment_status": "formative"
    }
  ],
  "practicals": [
    {
      "title": "Lab Practical",
      "source_text": "Lab activities to be done from the prescribed books (05 Marks)",
      "related_unit": "",
      "related_chapter": ""
    },
    {
      "title": "Geometric Constructions and Models",
      "source_text": "Constructing a square with a given side as described in the Baudhayana's Sulbasutras; Construct the circumcircle and circumcentre of a triangle; Practice forming logical converses... Engage in drawing, measurement and paper manipulation activities to discover geometric patterns involving triangles and 4-gons.",
      "related_unit": "Geometry",
      "related_chapter": "Introduction to Euclid's Geometry"
    },
    {
      "title": "Square Root Spiral Construction",
      "source_text": "Construct the square root spiral. Apply computational thinking to represent rational and irrational numbers through algorithms and visual models.",
      "related_unit": "Number System",
      "related_chapter": "Number System"
    },
    {
      "title": "Visualising Identities with Algebra Tiles",
      "source_text": "Visualising factorisation of quadratic expressions through algebra tiles and without using algebra tiles. Visualise algebraic identities using geometric models.",
      "related_unit": "Algebra",
      "related_chapter": "Exploring Algebraic Identities"
    }
  ],
  "activities": [
    {
      "title": "Tower of Hanoi Puzzle",
      "source_text": "Solve the Tower of Hanoi puzzle. Use computational thinking to identify patterns, write step-by-step rules.",
      "related_unit": "Algebra",
      "related_chapter": "Sequences and Progressions"
    },
    {
      "title": "Fractals Analysis",
      "source_text": "Analyse attributes of fractals using GP. Use two-dimensional representations of three-dimensional objects to visualise and solve problems.",
      "related_unit": "Algebra",
      "related_chapter": "Sequences and Progressions"
    },
    {
      "title": "Floor Plan Modelling",
      "source_text": "Represent a floor plan on a grid using coordinates. Apply computational thinking to model situations on the coordinate plane.",
      "related_unit": "Coordinate Geometry",
      "related_chapter": "Coordinate Geometry"
    },
    {
      "title": "Geometric Pattern Discovery",
      "source_text": "Engage in drawing, measurement and paper manipulation activities to discover geometric patterns involving triangles and 4-gons.",
      "related_unit": "Geometry",
      "related_chapter": "Quadrilaterals"
    },
    {
      "title": "Data Analysis Projects",
      "source_text": "Collect, organise, visualise and interpret data to answer a statistical investigative question. Use computational thinking strategies such as pattern recognition and simulation.",
      "related_unit": "Statistics and Probability",
      "related_chapter": "Statistics"
    },
    {
      "title": "Cultural Motifs Investigation",
      "source_text": "Identify cultural motifs involving circles, for example, the Dharmachakra, Ashoka Chakra, Sudarshan Chakra.",
      "related_unit": "Geometry",
      "related_chapter": "Circles"
    }
  ],
  "projects": [
    {
      "title": "Mathematical Modelling of Daily Life",
      "source_text": "Models daily-life phenomena and uses representations, such as graphs, tables, and equations to draw conclusions. Model linear growth and decay using linear polynomials.",
      "related_unit": "Algebra / Coordinate Geometry",
      "related_chapter": "Linear Equations in Two Variables"
    },
    {
      "title": "Historical Contributions Research",
      "source_text": "Recognises the important contributions made by mathematicians (Indian and others) in the field of Mathematics. List historical approximations to π.",
      "related_unit": "",
      "related_chapter": ""
    },
    {
      "title": "Cross-Disciplinary Applications",
      "source_text": "Applies mathematical knowledge and tools to analyse problems or situations across Science, Social Science, Visual Arts, Music, Vocational Education, and Sports.",
      "related_unit": "",
      "related_chapter": ""
    }
  ],
  "assessment_information": {
    "marks_distribution": [
      "Total Marks: 80 (External) + 20 (Internal)",
      "Unit I Number System: 7",
      "Unit II Algebra: 20",
      "Unit III Coordinate Geometry: 4",
      "Unit IV Geometry: 25",
      "Unit V Mensuration: 14",
      "Unit VI Statistics and Probability: 10",
      "Question Paper Typology (External): Remembering/Understanding - 43 Marks, Applying - 19 Marks, Analysing/Evaluating/Creating - 18 Marks",
      "Internal Assessment Total: 20 Marks"
    ],
    "excluded_or_non_assessed_content": [
      "None explicitly excluded in the provided text; all listed topics are part of the curriculum.",
      "Note on SSA congruence condition: 'Explain why SSA is not, in general, a valid congruence condition' implies it is non-assessed as a proof method but assessed for understanding its limitations."
    ],
    "teacher_notes": [
      "Curriculum redesigned in alignment with NEP 2020 and NCF-SE 2023.",
      "Prioritize deep conceptual understanding, logical reasoning, and competency-based education over rote learning.",
      "Integrate Indian Knowledge System (e.g., Baudhayana's Sulbasutras, Brahmagupta, Aryabhata) with contemporary knowledge.",
      "Emphasis on computational thinking: decomposing problems, algorithmic problem-solving, visualisation.",
      "Use of real-life applications and experiential learning is critical.",
      "Assessment includes continuous holistic assessment via projects, activities, and investigations.",
      "Prescribed Books for Lab Practical: 1. Mathematics - Textbook for class IX (NCERT), 2. Guidelines for Mathematics Laboratory in Schools (CBSE), 3. Laboratory Manual - Mathematics (NCERT), 4. Mathematics exemplar problems (NCERT).",
      "Time allocation for Question Paper Design: 3 Hours.",
      "Internal Assessment components: Pen Paper Test and Multiple Assessment (10 Marks), Portfolio (5 Marks), Lab Practical (5 Marks)."
    ]
  }
}
